mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of top-quartile PAYEMS growth months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises the equity risk premium and discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and increases in unemployment (UNRATE) confirm a contracting consumer demand cycle that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE increases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated, positive industrial production growth signals supply constraints that prompt additional Federal Reserve tightening, generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate reduces short-term liquidity and raises immediate borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above the prior quarter signals durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 quarterly change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month industrial production growth indicates rising operating leverage and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5% triggers recession risk pricing as corporate earnings expectations reset lower, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.
